reasoning:
Muqtada al-Sadr has a longstanding history of boycotting elections and later reversing his position, doing so most recently in August 2021 for the October 2021 elections. However, the timeline constraints for this resolution heavily suppress the probability of a "Yes". Although the election has been officially set for November 11, 2025, the resolution deadline is July 1, 2025 — less than two months from the forecast date. 

Sadr recently ordered his followers not to participate, calling the electoral process "defective." Further cementing this stance, on April 18, 2025, he publicly and formally rejected a plea from the Iraqi President to rejoin the elections, citing anti-corruption principles. Reversing this prominent, publicized stance within just ~10 weeks (before July 1) would make him appear indecisive and weak so soon after a major declaration. Historically, his un-boycotts happen very close to election day (e.g., ~6 weeks prior in 2021), suggesting any potential reversal for a November 2025 election would likely occur in September, well past this question's deadline. Additionally, late April sources report he is considering an "indirect path" to influence the election rather than direct participation, which would not satisfy the criteria of the party announcing it will "field candidates". Therefore, the probability of an official announcement before July 1, 2025, is quite low.
